title: Unity Software Q3 2024 Earnings Call - Key Excerpts
text: Strategic Transformation: CEO Matt Bromberg emphasized the company's focus on execution: We want to prioritize execution and discipline to drive more rapid product innovation and restore the strong authentic bond we have with our customers. The cancellation of the controversial runtime fee was a pivotal move, with Bromberg noting that 50 customers at their conference said they had initially blocked Unity 6 upgrades and were now green light after the pricing reversal. Financial Performance: The company exceeded guidance in Q3 2024. Revenue: $429 million (up 1% sequentially). Adjusted EBITDA: $92 million (vs. $75-80M guidance). Free cash flow: $115 million. Full-year guidance was raised to $1.703-1.708 billion in revenue. Product & Leadership Updates: Unity 6 achieved over 500,000 downloads following its launch, demonstrating strong early adoption. The company also announced new leadership including Steve Collins as CTO (formerly of King/Candy Crush) and Jarrod Yahes as incoming CFO. Growth Strategy: On the monetization side, Bromberg stated the company is conducting live testing of rebuilt machine learning models and seeing very good about where we are and the impact expected. Non-gaming markets showed promise with customers like KLM and Deutsche Bahn building enterprise applications on Unity's platform.
